C[apt1002: B B4 should have been D3. http://senseis.xmp.net/?BQM533 bottom diagram for B4. http://senseis.xmp.net/?44PointOneSpaceLowPincerInvasionInterception for D3.

apt1002: I considered hane-connect (i.e. B B4, W B5, B B3, W C5) which would have reverted to the ordinary joseki (after B D3, W E4, B F3). It would have made a nice W wall facing upwards, and would have given W sente to play e.g. O3. However, it gives B a nice big corner that is poking out along the bottom side, which coordinates quite well with Q3.

apt1002: I think W D3 (the actual move I played) will give a better result on balance. The black stone at C6 will live, but so will the white stone at C8, so the damage to the W's left side is not too bad. However, the damage to B's bottom side is huge. The final consideration is that B gets sente.
C[apt1002: I think B C2 should be B2.
C[apt1002: Ouch! I didn't see B E2. Nice move.
apt1002: Locally, W H3 looks best, though it probably starts a new group.
C[apt1002: The BL is still a few points better for W than the joseki.
apt1002: After B C17, W D18, B has an L-group. http://senseis.xmp.net/?LGroup
C[apt1002: Ooh. B B15 is nice. Not an L group after all. B will live.
apt1002: This is a complicated situation, but I can't find any way for B to live, so I am playing it out. I hope I'm right.
